headline: Trump lands in China for high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping
source: BBC News / The Guardian / Al Jazeera
summary: US President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing for a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Key issues on the agenda include tariffs, tech competition, the Iran war, and Taiwan. This marks the first visit by a US president to China since Trump's own visit in 2017.

headline: Hantavirus outbreak linked to cruise ship spreads across Europe
summary: A multi-country hantavirus outbreak linked to the cruise ship MV Hondius has hospitalized passengers in South Africa, the Netherlands, Germany, Saint Helena, Spain, France and Switzerland. A French patient is critically ill on an artificial lung. The outbreak originated from Andes hantavirus with illness onset between April 6-28, 2026.

headline: Ukrainian drone pilots warn NATO during military exercise in Sweden
summary: Ukrainian drone pilots turned a military exercise in Sweden into a critical warning demonstration for NATO. Meanwhile, Putin indicated willingness to meet Zelenskyy in a third country if a long-term peace deal is reached, though he condemned Western backing for Ukraine.

headline: Dutch hospital quarantines 12 staff after hantavirus protocol breach
summary: A Dutch hospital has quarantined 12 staff members as a preventive measure after blood and urine from a hantavirus patient were handled without observing strict protocols. Medics worldwide are working to stop the spread of the growing outbreak.

headline: Philippine Senate in lockdown after gunshots fired
summary: Police commandos entered the Philippine Senate building where a senator wanted by the International Criminal Court had sought refuge. Gunshots were fired, prompting a security lockdown of the legislative building.

headline: 85-year-old French widow detained in Trump's immigration crackdown
summary: An 85-year-old French widow was caught in President Trump's immigration crackdown and describes her detention. The case highlights the broad scope of recent immigration enforcement actions.

headline: Ghana to evacuate 300 citizens from South Africa over anti-immigrant protests
summary: Ghana announced plans to evacuate approximately 300 of its citizens from South Africa amid ongoing anti-immigrant protests and violence targeting foreign nationals.

headline: Waymo recalls thousands of robotaxis after vehicle swept into creek
summary: Thousands of Waymo autonomous vehicles have been recalled following an incident on April 20 where an empty robotaxi entered a flooded road in San Antonio, Texas and was swept into a creek.

headline: More than 1,000 cruise passengers quarantined in Bordeaux after illness outbreak
summary: Over 1,000 passengers are being held on a cruise ship docked in Bordeaux, France after 49 people fell ill from a gastrointestinal illness. The ship had set sail from Belfast on Friday.

headline: Trump's 'Golden Dome' missile defense estimated at $1.2 trillion
summary: President Trump's proposed 'Golden Dome' missile defense system is estimated to cost $1.2 trillion according to the Congressional Budget Office, far more than initially stated by the administration.
